crate_context.description: Core Jet bundler/build tool — CSS pipeline, build pipeline, dev server integration

specs.rationale: R5: CSS Pipeline requirement directly addresses PostCSS/Tailwind integration. Defines the CSS pipeline supporting concatenation, CSS Modules, PostCSS/Tailwind integration, and asset handling. Essential architectural reference for where Tailwind CSS fits in the production build pipeline.

Q6: dev-mode-watch-integration answer states 'Jet already has file-watch via the notify crate (dev_server module)'. This spec documents the watch infrastructure needed for CSS file monitoring and HMR reload in dev mode. Critical for watch integration requirement.

Package manager handles dependency resolution for lightningcss and Tailwind plugins. Referenced in pre-clarifications as the resolver that will identify and install plugin utilities. Context needed for integration points with package manager for version constraints.
Monorepo/workspace support. Tailwind CSS scanning must work across monorepo workspace packages. Understanding Jet's workspace detection helps scope CSS file scanning behavior in multi-package repos.

CSS pipeline output integrates with bundler's tree-shaking phase. Understanding DCE and side effects detection helps design how generated CSS rules interact with the overall pipeline minification strategy.

JavaScript minification. CSS minification is handled by lightningcss (per Q5 answer), not the JS mangler. Provides context on overall minification strategy but not directly relevant to CSS implementation.

Scope hoisting and module flattening for JavaScript. CSS pipeline is independent from JS hoisting optimization. Provides broader bundler context but minimal direct relevance to Tailwind CSS implementation.

Advanced package manager features (audit, patch, publish, workspace protocols). While workspace support is noted, this spec focuses on pnpm compatibility features less directly related to CSS pipeline implementation.
Bundle optimization via scope hoisting. CSS pipeline operates independently from JS scope analysis. Background context only.
